rulings.Case Title: MARK A. MENDAROS VS. DEUTSCHE BANK NATIONAL TRUST COMPANY ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR WITHDRAWAL OF ATTORNEY OF RECORD
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for July 14, 2020 line 3. PLAINTIFF MARK MENDAROS MOTION FOR WITHDRAWAL OF ATTORNEY OF RECORD READ THIS NOTIFICATION IN ITS ENTIRETY. Due to COVID-19 pandemic telephonic appearances are encouraged. If you appear in-person for your case notify the department by 5:00 p.m. one day before the hearing at Department501ContestTR@sftc.org. Department will follow social distancing guidelines and you will be required to wear a mask or a face covering. By 8:00 a.m. on the date of the hearing moving and opposing parties to submit electronic copies of their respective proposed orders (note: prevailing party to prepare an order repeating the tentative ruling verbatim in addition to any other proposed orders) in an editable format to Dept501Orders@sftc.org, so that the judge can modify it prior to signing, if needed. All communications shall include in the subject line the case name, number and hearing date. (e.g. "Smith v. Jones; CGC-18-555555; June 1, 2020"). All communications sent to the Court must be copied to the opposing party/counsel for whom e-mail addresses are known. The e-mail address provided herein shall only be used for the purposes indicated above. No unauthorized communications will be considered or responded to. Plaintiff's Motion for Withdrawal of Attorney of Record. is GRANTED. No opposition filed. Counsel to prepare order. =(501/CFH)

rulings.Case Title: CHERIE POWER VS. IGNACIO CONG FONG ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for July 14, 2020 line 4. DEFENDANT YEE HUNG, KUAI KEONG HONG & YEE LAN HUNG REVOCABLE TRUST, IGNACIO CONG FONG AKA KUAI KEONG HONG MOTION TO STRIKE 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT READ THIS NOTIFICATION IN ITS ENTIRETY. Due to COVID-19 pandemic telephonic appearances are encouraged. If you appear in-person for your case notify the department by 5:00 p.m. one day before the hearing at Department501ContestTR@sftc.org. Department will follow social distancing guidelines and you will be required to wear a mask or a face covering. By 8:00 a.m. on the date of the hearing moving and opposing parties to submit electronic copies of their respective proposed orders (note: prevailing party to prepare an order repeating the tentative ruling verbatim in addition to any other proposed orders) in an editable format to Dept501Orders@sftc.org, so that the judge can modify it prior to signing, if needed. All communications shall include in the subject line the case name, number and hearing date. (e.g. "Smith v. Jones; CGC-18-555555; June 1, 2020"). All communications sent to the Court must be copied to the opposing party/counsel for whom e-mail addresses are known. The e-mail address provided herein shall only be used for the purposes indicated above. No unauthorized communications will be considered or responded to. Defendants' Motion to Strike First Amended Complaint. is DENIED, 10 days to answer. First Amended Complaint sufficiently alleges facts to support a claim for punitive damages re: reckless disregard of Plaintiff's rights. =(501/CFH)

rulings.Case Title: TENDERLOIN NEIGHBORHOOD DEVELOPMENT CORPORATION VS. CAROLYN CURRY ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for July 14, 2020 line 7. DEFENDANT CAROLYN CURRY DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT READ THIS NOTIFICATION IN ITS ENTIRETY. Due to COVID-19 pandemic telephonic appearances are encouraged. If you appear in-person for your case notify the department by 5:00 p.m. one day before the hearing at Department501ContestTR@sftc.org. Department will follow social distancing guidelines and you will be required to wear a mask or a face covering. By 8:00 a.m. on the date of the hearing moving and opposing parties to submit electronic copies of their respective proposed orders (note: prevailing party to prepare an order repeating the tentative ruling verbatim in addition to any other proposed orders) in an editable format to Dept501Orders@sftc.org, so that the judge can modify it prior to signing, if needed. All communications shall include in the subject line the case name, number and hearing date. (e.g. "Smith v. Jones; CGC-18-555555; June 1, 2020"). All communications sent to the Court must be copied to the opposing party/counsel for whom e-mail addresses are known. The e-mail address provided herein shall only be used for the purposes indicated above. No unauthorized communications will be considered or responded to. Defendant's Demurrer to Complaint. is OVERRULED, 5 days to answer. Complaint sufficiently states a cause of action for unlawful detainer under that state and local laws. =(501/CFH)
